name: API Authentication Abuse and Backend Pivot
description: Attack tree modelling abuse of API authentication and authorization to pivot to backend systems
root_objective: Abuse API authentication weaknesses to access unauthorized data and pivot to backend services

nodes.title: Compromise Backend via API Abuse
nodes.description: Exploit API authentication and authorization weaknesses to access backend systems and sensitive data

nodes.title: Exploit Token-Based Auth Weaknesses
nodes.description: Abuse JWT, OAuth, or API key authentication mechanisms

nodes.title: JWT Algorithm Confusion Attack
nodes.description: Exploit JWT library accepting 'none' algorithm or confusing RSA with HMAC to forge tokens

nodes.title: Steal OAuth Tokens via Redirect Manipulation
nodes.description: Abuse open redirects in OAuth flow to steal authorization codes or access tokens

nodes.title: API Key Exposure in Client Code
nodes.description: Extract API keys from client-side JavaScript, mobile apps, or public repositories

nodes.title: Exploit Broken Object-Level Authorization
nodes.description: Access resources belonging to other users by manipulating object identifiers

nodes.title: Enumerate User Data via IDOR
nodes.description: Iterate through sequential or predictable resource IDs to access other users' data

nodes.title: Mass Assignment on User Properties
nodes.description: Modify protected user attributes by including them in API update requests

nodes.title: Exploit Service-to-Service Trust
nodes.description: Abuse trust relationships between microservices to access internal APIs

nodes.title: Access Internal APIs via SSRF
nodes.description: Use SSRF in a public-facing service to call internal microservice APIs that lack their own auth

nodes.title: Exploit GraphQL Introspection
nodes.description: Use enabled GraphQL introspection to discover hidden queries, mutations, and data types
